summary: Elementwise SGD on large float rows produced partial mismatches when each row was processed as one long vector. Flatten the tensor and split the kernel into fixed 1024-element tiles with explicit tail handling before applying Muls/Add/Sub.

error: op_host/sgd_custom_tbtgqxjmvnjdppkb20260310050603.cpp:11:59: error: cannot convert ‘const gert::StorageShape*’ to ‘const gert::Shape*’ in initialization
summary: When refactoring tiling from row/col metadata to flat-length metadata, keep TilingFunc input typed as gert::StorageShape*. Read totalLength with inputShape->GetOriginShape().GetShapeSize() instead of assigning GetInputShape(0) to gert::Shape*.

For AscendC vector math, avoid reusing the same local tensor as both destination and source across chained Add/Muls steps in the SGD update. Write the momentum result to one temp buffer and the accumulated step to a second temp buffer before scaling and subtraction.
